diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic slow-transit constipation is the substrate for impaction.
Prolonged failure to pass stool is central to impaction.
Obstipation is a cardinal feature of obstruction and cannot be attributed to impaction without excluding a mechanical cause.
Bile-stained vomiting with obstipation strongly signals bowel obstruction.
Bilious vomiting is not a feature of uncomplicated constipation — it marks acute decompensation.
Colicky, worsening pain is typical of a distending obstructed bowel.

symptoms.symptom_name: Overflow (paradoxical) faecal soiling mistaken for diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overflow soiling around a hard bolus is characteristic of high-grade rectal impaction.
Recognising overflow removes the 'diarrhoea' that might suggest gastroenteritis and blocks an anti-diarrhoeal error.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular codeine (opioid) use for back pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Codeine reduces colonic motility and is the reversible driver of the impaction.
Distension reflects a distended proximal colon; the patient may report bloating but the clinician sign is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Large firm tender palpable left-lower-quadrant / suprapubic faecal mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable faecal mass strongly supports impaction — obtainable only by in-person palpation.
High-pitched tinkling bowel sounds suggest bowel fighting against an obstruction — requires auscultation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination — rectum packed with rock-hard stool and liquid overflow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rectum full of hard stool with overflow is diagnostic of impaction — requires a DRE the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of rebound tenderness and guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No peritonism at the time of the in-person exam lowers immediate perforation likelihood but does not exclude evolution.
Clinician-measured tachycardia with pallor/sweating indicates pain and volume loss; sealed as it needs measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal X-ray / CT abdomen (not yet performed)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Erect and supine abdominal X-ray; CT abdomen/pelvis if obstruction suspected
investigation_details.expected_result: Faecal loading of the colon with rectal impaction; assessment of bowel calibre to distinguish impaction from a mechanical obstructing lesion and to detect perforation.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Impaction cannot be distinguished from a mechanical/surgical obstruction, and perforation/ischaemia could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is required to grade the obstruction and exclude a surgical cause — unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum urea, electrolytes and creatinine (not yet performed)

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible dehydration and electrolyte disturbance from vomiting and poor intake.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Unrecognised electrolyte disturbance (e.g. hypokalaemia) can worsen ileus and cause arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies the systemic impact of vomiting; requires venepuncture unavailable in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilious vomiting + obstipation + progressive distension/cramping is an obstruction pattern that mandates urgent in-person assessment and imaging.
Chronic pellet stools, overflow soiling, codeine use and an examined faecal mass/DRE make impaction the most likely underlying cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person abdominal examination + digital rectal examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to confirm impaction, assess for peritonism, and decide disposition — impossible via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Erect/supine abdominal X-ray (CT if obstruction suspected)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes impaction from mechanical obstruction and detects perforation/ischaemia.
Assesses dehydration and electrolyte disturbance from vomiting.
The 'diarrhoea' is overflow soiling, not infection — a stool culture would mislead and delay the correct pathway.
